title: shape_expr representation rule
description: Canonical representation rule for shape_expr fields appearing in algorithm.resolved.yaml (temporaries[].shape_expr, state_variables[].shape_expr) and derived_contract.json (io_contract.inputs[].shape_expr, io_contract.outputs[].shape_expr). The validator tools/validate_pipeline_semantics.py loads this schema at module init and rejects any value that does not match one of the oneOf branches. Function-call style notations such as vector(N), matrix(M,N), tensor are explicitly forbidden; use [N] or (M,N) instead.

oneOf.title: scalar literal
oneOf.description: The literal token 'scalar' (case-insensitive). Use for 0-dimensional / single-value temporaries.

oneOf.title: bracket list form
oneOf.description: Comma-separated dimensions enclosed in square brackets. Each dimension token must be either an integer literal (\d+) or an identifier-style symbol ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*). Function-call notation, parens, and other punctuation inside dimension tokens are forbidden. Example: [3], [nx, ny], [Nz, Nx, Ny].

oneOf.title: paren tuple form
oneOf.description: Comma-separated dimensions enclosed in parentheses. Each dimension token must be either an integer literal (\d+) or an identifier-style symbol ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*). Function-call notation, parens, and other punctuation inside dimension tokens are forbidden. Example: (d1,d2), (nx, ny, nz).

x-forbidden-examples-note: Single-element paren tuples such as (tensor) or (d1) are NOT forbidden; they are valid 1-D paren-tuple shapes whose single dimension token is an identifier. Forbidden cases above all involve function-call notation (vector(N), matrix(M,N)), float/arithmetic literals, or empty/malformed tokens.
